metadata.title: Glassmorphism Generator — CSS Glass Effect Builder
metadata.description: Create stunning glassmorphism CSS effects. Customize backdrop blur, transparency, borders, and colors with live preview. Copy ready-to-use CSS.
metadata.keywords: glassmorphism generator, glass effect css, backdrop blur, frosted glass css, glassmorphism css, glass ui, transparent card css, blur effect generator, glass morphism tool, css glass card

heading: Glassmorphism Generator
subheading: Create frosted glass UI effects with customizable blur, opacity, and borders. Live preview on a vibrant background.

seo.section1Text: Glassmorphism is a UI design trend that creates a frosted glass effect using CSS backdrop-filter. Elements appear translucent with a blurred background showing through, creating depth and visual hierarchy. Popularized by Apple's macOS and iOS designs, it combines transparency, blur, and subtle borders.
seo.section2Text: The core properties are backdrop-filter: blur() for the frosted effect, a semi-transparent background-color for the tint, and a subtle border for edge definition. Adjust the blur radius for more or less transparency, and use background saturation to enhance colors visible through the glass.
seo.section3Text: The backdrop-filter property is supported in all modern browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge. For older browsers, provide a fallback solid background color. Use @supports (backdrop-filter: blur(1px)) to conditionally apply the glass effect.
seo.section4List: Use on vibrant or gradient backgrounds for the best visual impact | Keep blur between 8-20px for readable text behind glass | Add a subtle white or light border for edge definition | Ensure text contrast meets WCAG accessibility guidelines | Layer multiple glass panels at different opacities for depth | Combine with subtle box-shadow for extra elevation

ui.howItWorksText: Adjust blur, opacity, and border settings to create your glass effect. The preview shows the result on a colorful background. Copy the CSS when ready.
